clusters.title: Access-log formatting can read past caller-provided C key buffers

member_findings.title: Access-log formatting can read past caller-provided C key buffers
member_findings.impact: When a C API call supplies key buffers with explicit lengths but a missing length array on the new lazy access-log path, the operation path skips consuming the nested keys, but the recorder still reaches objectKeysToString and falls back to strlen(cKey[i]). A non-NUL-terminated caller buffer can make logging scan past the declared key memory and persist adjacent process memory, including sensitive data, into access logs.
member_findings.fix: Do not fall back to strlen for APIs whose buffers are length-delimited. Require cKeyLens for this overload, skip logging when it is null, or pass an empty bounded representation; also guard OCPut so NestedKeysRef is called only when both cNestedObjectKeys and cNestedObjKeyLenArray are present.

clusters.title: Sampled-out access records still construct the full object access state

member_findings.title: Sampled-out access records still construct the full object access state
member_findings.impact: Every hot object access call now creates an ObjectAccessRecorder whose embedded ObjectAccessState contains many strings and std::function members, even when access_sample_rate drops the record immediately. Call sites such as KVClient::Get still instantiate the facade unconditionally before the sampler can return false, so sampled-out traffic avoids formatting but still pays repeated constructor/destructor and cache-footprint cost on the foreground request path.
member_findings.fix: Make the sampled-out facade genuinely lightweight: split the disabled recorder from the full state, store ObjectAccessState only behind a lazily constructed inline/optional state after shouldRecord_ is true, or add a cheap guard/factory that returns before building the full field state on dropped records.

clusters.title: Access sampling decision logic is duplicated across two entrypoints

member_findings.title: Access sampling decision logic is duplicated across two entrypoints
member_findings.impact: ShouldRecordAccessType repeats the same snapshot, REQUEST_OUT, full-rate, request-sampled-in, zero-rate, trace-hash, and event-sampling ladder implemented just above in ShouldRecordAccess. The two paths are used by different access-recording call sites, so future changes to access sampling semantics can easily update one path and leave the other with divergent behavior.
member_findings.fix: Extract the common access decision into one helper that accepts AccessKeyType and the loaded snapshot, then have ShouldRecordAccess only map AccessRecorderKey to AccessKeyType before delegating.

clusters.title: Runtime sampler flag transaction path lacks coverage

member_findings.title: Runtime sampler flag transaction path lacks coverage
member_findings.impact: The PR adds a separate dynamic-config path for request/access/diagnostic sample rates that validates a batch, applies gflags transactionally, rolls back on failure, and preserves explicit-state semantics. The new tests exercise LogSampler::UpdateConfigFromFlags and proto updates directly, but they do not drive Flags::UpdateFlagParameter through this branch, so regressions in parsing, partial commit rollback, or request-only derivation from actual runtime flag maps would not fail any test.
member_findings.fix: Add a focused flags unit test that calls Flags::UpdateFlagParameter with sampler flag maps, asserting effective LogSampler ppm values for request-only derivation, explicit access/diagnostic overrides, and an invalid mixed batch that leaves both the gflag values and LogSampler snapshot at the previous-good config.

clusters.title: Expire master failure exits without recording the access result

member_findings.title: Expire master failure exits without recording the access result
member_findings.impact: When ExpireFromMaster returns an error inside the retry loop, the function now returns before the new ObjectAccessRecorder reaches the only Record() call at the end of the function. The previous code explicitly recorded that failure on this path, so failed Expire requests can now lose their access/performance result and only trigger the generic destructor warning.
member_findings.fix: Record the error before the early return, or wrap the recorder in a local RAII finalizer that always calls Result(last_rc).Record() before any return after the recorder is created.
